column4: Shielded copper cable with concentric conductors used for broadband, radio-frequency, television, and early long-distance telephone transmission.
Electromechanical switching systems that connected calls without manual switchboard operators.
Line-of-sight radio relay networks carrying telephone, television, and data traffic between towers.
Technique that carries multiple communication channels simultaneously by assigning each signal to a separate frequency band.
Technique that interleaves multiple digital channels into repeating time slots over a shared transmission link.
Computer networks connecting devices across a room, building, campus, or factory using shared media and packet protocols.
Wired local-area networking family that uses framed packet communication over shared or switched links.
Distributed naming system that maps human-readable internet names to network addresses and service records.
Interdomain routing protocol that lets autonomous systems exchange reachability information across the global internet.
Application protocol for transferring hypertext, APIs, metadata, and web resources across the internet.
Client software for retrieving, rendering, navigating, and scripting web documents and applications.
Packet voice systems that encode speech and signaling over internet protocol networks.
Radio access networks that divide service areas into cells and hand off mobile users between base stations.
Second-generation digital cellular standard using SIM identity, digital voice coding, roaming, and circuit-switched mobile services.
All-IP mobile broadband networks using OFDMA radio access, evolved packet core, and high-throughput cellular data service.
Cellular radio and core-network standard supporting enhanced mobile broadband, low-latency services, and massive machine-type communication.
Optical multiplexing technique that carries many wavelength channels through a single fiber to scale backbone capacity.
Undersea optical cable systems with repeaters, landing stations, and management systems for intercontinental internet backbones.
Broadband access systems that transmit packet data over hybrid fiber-coaxial cable television networks.
Digital broadband access technology that carries high-frequency data over existing twisted-pair telephone loops.
Protocol and tooling for monitoring, configuring, and managing network devices using structured management information.
Network architecture separating control and forwarding planes so software controllers can program packet-forwarding behavior.
Internet protocol for synchronizing computer clocks across packet-switched networks.
